segments.text: When Samuel grew old, he appointed his sons as judges over Israel.
The name of his firstborn son was Joel, and the name of his second was Abijah. They were judges in Beersheba.
But his sons did not walk in his ways; they turned aside toward dishonest gain, accepting bribes and perverting justice.
So all the elders of Israel gathered together and came to Samuel at Ramah.
Look,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they said,
you are old, and your sons do not walk in your ways. Now appoint a king to judge us like all the other nations.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But when they said,
Give us a king to judge us,
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: their demand was displeasing in the sight of Samuel; so he prayed to the LORD.
And the LORD said to Samuel,
Listen to the voice of the people in all that they say to you. For it is not you they have rejected, but they have rejected Me as their king.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Just as they have done from the day I brought them up out of Egypt until this day, forsaking Me and serving other gods, so they are doing to you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Now listen to them, but you must solemnly warn them and show them the manner of the king who will reign over them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So Samuel spoke all the words of the LORD to the people who were asking him for a king.
He said,
This will be the manner of the king who will reign over you: He will take your sons and appoint them to his own chariots and horses, to run in front of his chariots.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: He will appoint some for himself as commanders of thousands and of fifties, and others to plow his ground, to reap his harvest, to make his weapons of war, and to equip his chariots.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And he will take your daughters to be perfumers, cooks, and bakers.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: He will take the best of your fields and vineyards and olive groves and give them to his servants.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: He will take a tenth of your grain and grape harvest and give it to his officials and servants.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And he will take your menservants and maidservants and your best cattle and donkeys and put them to his own use.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: He will take a tenth of your flocks, and you yourselves will become his slaves.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: When that day comes, you will beg for relief from the king you have chosen, but the LORD will not answer you on that day.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Nevertheless, the people refused to listen to Samuel.
No!
segments.speaker: People of Israel

segments.text: they said.
We must have a king over us.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then we will be like all the other nations, with a king to judge us, to go out before us, and to fight our battles.
segments.speaker: People of Israel

segments.text: Samuel listened to all the words of the people and repeated them in the hearing of the LORD.
Listen to their voice,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: the LORD said to Samuel.
Appoint a king for them.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Then Samuel told the men of Israel,
Everyone must go back to his city.
segments.speaker: Samuel
